evidence_excerpt: Disease advanced to CLIS with complete loss of voluntary movement (ALSFRS-R = 0), later physical status worsened after acute pneumonia, and 4 months after the online experiment there was no evidence of consciousness or alertness.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Mechanical ventilator and PEG were needed (late 2013); remained hospitalized for 2 years; no communication with family for more than one year due to complete paralysis.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: In late 2013, mechanical ventilator and transcutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy were needed.
By early 2015, all voluntary motor functions including ocular movement and eye blinking were completely lost (CLIS).
Online EEG-based endogenous BCI achieved 87.5% accuracy enabling yes/no communication during the third visit (2016).
Physical status worsened after recovering from acute pneumonia, preventing follow-up experiments.
Four months after the online experiment, ERP testing showed no evidence of consciousness or alertness.

notes_on_uncertainty: The definitive index time is approximated as the ALS diagnosis date (Nov 2012). Exact dates for pneumonia and the loss of alertness are relative to visit schedule; no death is documented. The BCI-enabled communication represents transient functional improvement rather than sustained recovery.
